titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Yale Student Composers Interviews
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Anne Rhodes

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday April 3, 2020 at 00:00

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Yale Student Composers project documents Yale's celebrated graduate student composers. These promising young artists are interviewed at the outset of their studies at Yale, and they speak about their work, musical education, inspiration, and other subjects. Their testimony is filmed by a professional videographer.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: The following interviews, originally in this collection, have been moved to Major Figures of American Music (OHV): Cerrone, Chris - OHVII 1 a (now OHV 410); Adams, Samuel - OHVII 2 a (now OHV 411).

accruals.head: Accruals
accruals.p: OHAM conducts interviews to add to this collection on a yearly basis.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: OHVII, Yale Student Composers Interviews, Oral History of American Music, in the Music Library of Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research. Streaming access to video and sound recordings in this collection is available when connected to the Yale network, or otherwise by request.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged alphabetically by surname of interviewee.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Rights and permissions for OHAM materials vary between individual interviews. Researchers wishing to publish or broadcast OHAM materials are responsible for obtaining direct authorization from copyright holders: generally, the interviewees or their estates.
